calendar.matters:
Petitioners Notice Of Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-22-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER MARY AYERS' Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Ayers' petition to vacate arbitration award is denied and the court confirms the award pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1286 ["If a petition or response under this chapter is duly served and filed, the court shall confirm the award as made, whether rendered in this state or another state, unless in accordance with this chapter it corrects the award and confirms it as corrected, vacates the award or dismisses the proceeding."] (See Law Offices of David S. Karton v. Segreto (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 1, 8 [court must confirm arbitration award if it does not vacate or correct it or dismiss petition]; Louise Gardens of Encino Homeowners' Assn., Inc. v. Truck Ins. Exchange, Inc. (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 648, 658 [same].)

"The party seeking to vacate an arbitration award bears the burden of establishing that one of the six grounds listed in section 1286.2 applies and that the party was prejudiced by the arbitrator's error." (Royal All Assocs., Inc. v. Liebhaber (2016) 2 Cal.App.5th 1092, 1106.) Ayers fails to demonstrate that the award was obtained by corruption, the arbitrator was corrupt, the arbitrator engaged in misconduct, the arbitrator exceeded her authority, the arbitrator failed to disclose a ground for disqualification, or the arbitrator should have disqualified herself. The Eisenberg declaration dated July 21, 2020 and the documents that Ayers asks the court to judicially notice fail to demonstrate that the arbitrator reviewed an improper ex parte communication or based her decision on that review. Ayers' motion to vacate is based on pure conjecture. The record indicates that the e-mail that included the Dropbox file or link that contained the article entitled "Business Models, Business Strategy and Innovation" was the result of a computer "hack" of the arbitrator's email, and that the arbitrator never opened the attachment and had no knowledge of its contents. (See Clause Decl. 18 & Ex. P; see A.M. Classic Const., Inc. v. Tri-Build Development Co. (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 1470 ["In the absence of a showing that the arbitrator was improperly influenced or actually considered evidence outside the original arbitration proceedings," amended award issued in response to ex parte communication was not procured by corruption, fraud, undue means, or misconduct of the arbitrator, and did not exceed the arbitrator's powers].) Ayers similarly fails to show that the arbitrator divulged Ayers' confidential medical information or that the arbitrator created an adverse relationship vis-a-vis Ayers. A party cannot file or threaten a lawsuit against a judicial officer or arbitrator to disqualify that individual from presiding over the dispute. (See First W. Dev. Corp. v. Superior Court (1989) 212 Cal.App.3d 860, 867; Greenspan v. LADT, LLC (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 1413, 1459.)

The court further rejects Ayers' newly-minted and untimely argument that the parties never had a binding agreement to arbitrate. Ayers' petition to vacate admits that the parties entered into an agreement to arbitrate and she is bound by those judicial admissions. (See Braverman v. Rosenthal (1951) 102 Cal.App.2d 30, 32 [judicial admissions conclusive]; Valerio v. Andrew Youngquist Constr. (2002) 103 Cal. App. 4th 1264, 1271 [admissions in answer established contract]; Ayers' Petition to Vacate, pars. 2, 4(a) and (c), 5, and 6.)

Order signed (one order as to both the motion to vacate arbitration award and the motion to confirm arbitration award).

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Oct-22-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER MARY AYERS' DEMURRER.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Ayers' demurrer is off calendar. Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41(a) provides that "[b]efore filing a demurrer pursuant to this chapter, the demurring party shall meet and confer in person or by telephone with the party who filed the pleading that is subject to demurrer" (emphasis added.) The undisputed evidence demonstrates that Ayers did not comply with this requirement as her counsel allegedly only sent a letter to Kaiser. (Eisenberg Decl., par. 2 (9/16/20.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Hearing Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-22-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: RESPONDENTS KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN, INC., THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC., LESLIE AIKEN, MD, AND MEAGHAN LYNCH, MD's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Granted, per ruling on Motion to vacate. The court confirms the award pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1286.

Order signed (one order as to both the motion to vacate arbitration award and the motion to confirm arbitration award).

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Oct-19-2020 Continued To Oct-22-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
